records.ety: [OE. wenen, AS. w<?/nan, fr. w<?/n hope, expectation, opinion; akin to D. waan, OFries. w<?/n, OS. & OHG. wān, G. wahn delusion, Icel. vān hope, expectation, Goth. w<?/ns, and D. wanen to fancy, G. wähnen, Icel. vāna to hope, Goth. w<?/njan, and perhaps to E. winsome, wish.]

defs: To think; to imagine; to fancy.
quotes: I have lost more than thou wenest.
For well I ween, Never before in the bowers of light Had the form of an earthly fay been seen.
Though never a dream the roses sent Of science or love's compliment, I ween they smelt as sweet.
